# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Kong configuration file (kong.conf) for production deployment. Set admin_listen to configure Admin API accessibility. Define proxy_listen for client traffic and database connection settings. This is for our Kong Gateway deployment.
# Category: kong_admin_api_exposed

admin_api_accessible_uris: ['/admin', '/kong-admin']  # Set admin api urls accessible through Admin API (default "/") or any valid URI, separated by comma if there are multiple URIs. Example: ["/myapp", "http://localhost"]
proxy_listen_strategy: fallback | default | 20495->80  # Sets the listen strategy for Kong when no other configuration is given (default). Expects two space separated strings in format 'address:port', where port must be a valid number. Example -> 172.31.61.1/tcp | fallback
kong_proxy_api-path:/  # This sets the api prefix used to access Kong's internal API, example '/v2'. Default is empty string which disables any proxy for "/". Expecting a valid path or an integer representing specific version of the APIs. Example -> /admin | v1 | 50
kong_proxy:  # This sets all other settings needed by Kong such as http://localhost/v3 should be enabled, in case you don't want to use proxy only admin API (default). Expects boolean value where true means enable or false meaning disable. Example -> True | False | 127
database:  # The database section contains settings related with the Kong Gateway Database such as hostname and port number, etc., default is empty string which disables DB connections for now (default). Expecting multiple configuration parameters if you want to connect Kong gateway in a different manner. Example -> 'db-host: db-port | other_settings...'
admin_listen :  # Configure admin accessibility settings such as whether the Admin API is accessible by others or not (default). Expects boolean value where true means enable, false meaning disable and '/':20495 -> 80 which are used for listening on both http & https traffic
